summary: Completed comprehensive testing of P1 priority features: Profile editing UI, Minutes search, Distributions search, and mobile table scroll. All 13 backend tests passed (100%). All frontend UI tests passed.

profile_update_api: PUT /api/auth/profile rejects request with no fields (400)
minutes_search_api: Search works together with type filter (minutes_type param)
distributions_search_api: Search works with status filter (status param)
Search works with purpose filter (purpose param)
frontend_ui: Settings page loads correctly with data-testid='settings-page'
Edit button appears next to profile name (data-testid='edit-profile-btn')
Clicking Edit shows form with Display Name input (data-testid='profile-name-input')
Cancel button closes form without saving changes
Save button updates profile name and shows 'Profile updated' toast
Minutes page has type filter buttons (data-testid='filter-all', 'filter-meeting', etc.)
Minutes search triggers debounced API call
Distributions page has status filter buttons (data-testid='filter-all', 'filter-approved', etc.)
Distributions search triggers debounced API call
Distributions table has overflow-x-auto class for mobile scroll

seed_data_creation: Used 'USE DEMO DATA' button to create Family Trust 2024 with sample minutes and distributions
context_for_next_testing_agent: P1 features fully tested: 1) Profile editing works via PUT /api/auth/profile with UI at Settings page showing Edit button, name input, Save/Cancel buttons. 2) Minutes search uses debounced query parameter sent to GET /api/minutes?search=term&trust_id=xxx. 3) Distributions search uses same pattern via GET /api/distributions?search=term&trust_id=xxx. 4) Mobile tables have overflow-x-auto class. Type and status filters still work alongside search.

ui_elements.edit_button: data-testid='edit-profile-btn' - appears next to user name

minutes_search.behavior: Debounced search (300ms) triggers server-side API call

distributions_search.behavior: Debounced search (300ms) triggers server-side API call

mobile_table_scroll.implementation: Tables wrapped in div with 'card-trust overflow-x-auto' classes
mobile_table_scroll.result: Table is horizontally scrollable on mobile
